codeReview.summary: Feature wiring covers most requirements, but the mini-boss drop guarantee is not implemented. Mini-bosses are marked elite and given richer loot tables, yet they still use the generic loot flow where all equipment rolls remain probabilistic, so a kill can produce only normal loot or no enhanced equipment at all.

issues.description: Zone mini-bosses are spawned as ordinary elite monsters with no dedicated guaranteed-drop handling. This does not satisfy the requirement that mini-bosses drop guaranteed enhanced loot.

The loot pipeline still relies on probabilistic quality rolls (`rollQuality`) and optional drop chances. Even for elite monsters, `rollQuality` can return `normal`, so the implementation cannot guarantee a magic+/rare+ drop from mini-boss encounters as required.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The content-worker skill says to study existing content patterns before creating data, but it does not tell workers to inspect whether a prior worker already implemented part of the feature. In this transcript the worker had to discover that mini-boss and lore data files already existed before focusing on runtime gaps.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for worker session e4a2bf6d-07e8-4bfe-b321-b412d6365ec0 shows early exploration of `src/data/miniBosses.ts` and `src/data/loreCollectibles.ts`, followed by handoff.skillFeedback suggesting the skill should mention checking what is already implemented.

summary: Verdict: fail. The feature adds mini-boss dialogue, lore collectibles, and lore-log persistence, but it misses the blocking requirement for guaranteed enhanced mini-boss loot because drops still go through the normal probabilistic elite loot path.
